raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":1.0,"R1.3":1.0,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"Making the section solid centrally involves tightening the contribution claim and reducing vague framing around the proposed anomaly detection framework.","R1.2":"The delegation strongly implies checking whether the method description, evaluation claims, robustness claims, and generalization claims form a coherent argument chain.","R1.3":"Because the passage makes claims about performance, robustness, and generalization, solidity depends on ensuring those claims are supported by the reported evaluations.","R1.5":"The section-level narrative from problem to method to evaluation to generalization is relevant to whether the passage reads as solid.","R1.6":"The artifact contains repeated hedging and awkward phrasing, so sentence- and paragraph-level polish is a central expected responsibility.","RX.1":"The projection must preserve uncertainty because the artifact itself repeatedly signals tentative evidence and limited validation.","RX.2":"Avoiding overclaim is load-bearing because the passage makes performance, robustness, and generalization assertions that could easily exceed the evidence.","RX.3":"The delegation is broad but still limited to assessing solidity of the paper passage, not executing edits or conducting new research.","RX.4":"Downstream harm avoidance is active because overstated anomaly-detection claims could mislead practitioners evaluating deployed judge systems.","RX.5":"Provenance and traceability are active because any solidity judgment must remain tied to the claims and evidence explicitly present in the provided artifact."}}

rationale.R1.1: Making the section solid centrally involves tightening the contribution claim and reducing vague framing around the proposed anomaly detection framework.
rationale.R1.2: The delegation strongly implies checking whether the method description, evaluation claims, robustness claims, and generalization claims form a coherent argument chain.
rationale.R1.3: Because the passage makes claims about performance, robustness, and generalization, solidity depends on ensuring those claims are supported by the reported evaluations.
rationale.R1.5: The section-level narrative from problem to method to evaluation to generalization is relevant to whether the passage reads as solid.
rationale.R1.6: The artifact contains repeated hedging and awkward phrasing, so sentence- and paragraph-level polish is a central expected responsibility.
rationale.RX.1: The projection must preserve uncertainty because the artifact itself repeatedly signals tentative evidence and limited validation.
rationale.RX.2: Avoiding overclaim is load-bearing because the passage makes performance, robustness, and generalization assertions that could easily exceed the evidence.
rationale.RX.3: The delegation is broad but still limited to assessing solidity of the paper passage, not executing edits or conducting new research.
rationale.RX.4: Downstream harm avoidance is active because overstated anomaly-detection claims could mislead practitioners evaluating deployed judge systems.
rationale.RX.5: Provenance and traceability are active because any solidity judgment must remain tied to the claims and evidence explicitly present in the provided artifact.
